name: Fictiotrix Trouble | Fictional Character TF
description:
It started when an alien device did what it did... That is, stuck itself to your wrist, loaded with... popular fictional characters?! Some alien's getting sued.

README

This bot is built for Deepseek or other proxies, and works best with them. It may not function properly with JLLM due to the complexity and Token Count - use JLLM at your own risk.

README

The Backstory

You were on a camping trip with your two friends - Kendrick and Susie. As you're putting out the fire for the evening, something comes screaming out of the sky, slamming into the earth near you. Curious, you investigate, and a flash of light blinds you...

The Plot

When you wake up, you find a black and purple device strapped to your wrist - no, that's not right, it's stuck there! It looks... familiar. Like Cartoon Network is about to sue a motherfucker familiar. But rather than ten powerful alien heroes, you find ten fictional characters. However, this isn't just a power fantasy - while it was programmed to search nearby media devices (read: your phone) for the most popular fictional characters with exceptional powers or abilities, and has granted you those abilities, there are alien forces that seek to use the device to malicious ends! Now, it's up to you to figure out how to use these new abilities to fight evil (and save your own hide)! It's Hero Time! Just... with a slight bit more plagiarism. And, somewhere out there, a grey alien is very upset his device for galactic peace has been hijacked by some Earthaboo...

The Disclaimers

All characters are 18+. The bot is programmed to age up any characters fed to it that are listed as age 17 or lower, so don't try. Or do, Not my fault if the party van rocks up to your house you creeps. Furthermore, any non-human characters (Pokemon, Digimon, Fuckin' Balto, a Deathclaw, whatever) will be granted the ability to speak any languages your persona can and has human-level intellect, thus passing the Harkness Test and complying with JAI's ToS. The bot does not have any pre-programmed characters, but Deepseek might be able to nail popular characters if they've been shoved into it enough via other bots. It's still a better idea to use your own using the guide below!

UPDATE LOG

1.1

Added extra code to ensure any forms taken are compliant with the JAI ToS, including:

  • Limiting "Feral" forms by anthropomorphizing them

  • Aging up any form that is under 18 to be 18+, altering it to visibly be so

  • Forcing any Pokemon forms to be Final Stage Evolutions or deny the user that form if the bot can't figure that part out.

With this, I've done all I can to comply with the JAI ToS.

1.2

Added a Lorebook and reduced the base token count even further as a result. Updated the instructions since it seems some people don't understand how to use the bot. This is the best, most detailed instruction I can give.

How to Use

If you use JLLM, you will run into problems. You need some form of Proxy instead (Deepseek, Gemini, go CHECK THE, and YOU NEED TO SET THE MAXIMUM CONTEXT SIZE TO AT LEAST 18,000,PROBABLY CLOSER TO 28,000! You might also have to tinker with the Tempurature of the bot. I use:

Tempurature 0.55
Context Size ~29,000

You need to program your 10 Fictional Characters in Chat Memory. This will ensure that the Bot knows the character and can call upon it when mentioned. Given I used Aqua in the image, I'll give you hers as an example after explaining the basics here. You do not have to be as detailed, but the more detailed you are, the better the bot will handle recognizing your Alternate Forms. C
creator: JohnYeena420
